title: What an American thinks about Chileans

transcript.text: Okay, this video is what I think about Chile. It's not going to be anything negative. It's too easy to say negative stuff about people. I'm going to say Chile because it's the only country I was in for five years, for a really long time, other than America. I've been in a few countries for like six months and stuff, but it's the country I was in for a long time.

So, you know, it's easy to talk trash. A lot of like expatriates like to talk trash about countries that they've been in, and they forget that, you know, well, they've been in that country, so maybe they didn't like the country they come from. And I could talk trash about my own country, too, forever.
But, uh, um, but like basically what I think about Chile, um, let's see, Chileans, uh, Chileans have a different idea about money, I think.
Like where Chilean has extra money, I've seen that they'll spend like all of it like on a party. And that's cool, spend all your money on a party for your friends.
Um, instead of, you know, hoard it. Um, and also, uh, I went, uh, to this place called Termas del Flacco. Is that what it's called?
Um, Hot Springs town, like where you leave to go hike to that plane that crashed and it wouldn't be alive.

And I went there with my buddy and it wasn't in, uh, it wasn't in tour season and there was this lady in a hotel and she just let us stay there for free because she wasn't like in tour season money making mode, you know.
And that was cool. Um, and then my buddy went back like a year, like a week later with his girlfriend and she let them stay there for free again, too.
So, and, um, you know, I bought stuff from people off the street, you know, and they'd be like, oh yeah, and they'll throw you in a little bit extra thing in there, you know.
So, you know, not totally like cut and dry and cold calculated like us. And we can learn from that for shizzle and also time. They're not so stratified about time either.
Um, um, you know, like, like my wife, her, she has like a first cousin who she hadn't seen for like seven years.
She lives like seven city blocks away, town block. And, uh, and then, you know, she came over one day, like there was nothing.
And then they started hanging out every day for a couple of days. So that's pretty cool.
You know, and I've, I've noticed that like somebody will call you up or you'll call somebody up that you hadn't seen for like years, you know, and it's like no time went by at all.

Like time doesn't really exist for them. Um, so there's a lot more patients like in America, if you don't talk to somebody for, you know, more than a few months, it's kind of like you would feel awkward about calling them up again.
Cause it's kind of like, oh, I guess I don't hang out with them anymore. So, you know, like Chileans aren't really like that.
They're kind of like, they kind of don't really think about time like that. Like, they'll call you just cause they feel like it, you know? Um, and, um, um, and they're really good at forgiving.

They'll forgive you really quickly too. Um, like here in America, if somebody pisses somebody else, it's kind of like, uh, um, and then you see them again, you're kind of like, uh, right over in Chile, they'll be like, you know,

They'll act like nothing happened, you know? And they'll be like, Hey, how's it going? And, uh, you know, like with my wife, we'd get in fights and then like two seconds later, we'd be like, Hey, yeah, how's it going? You know?

And, uh, that's cool. It's definitely something to learn from. I'm not Chilean. Um, I mean, there are, I guess, some negative stuff too that would result in them not having as much being as prosperous as us, but, um, I don't, I'm not going to really talk about that kind of stuff.

Um, I don't think it's, it does any use. So let's see what else, um, um, definitely more hot blooded. I remember Arnold Schwarzenegger got in trouble because he said that like Latinos were hot blooded.
Um, he didn't know he's being recorded and he got in trouble and accused of being racist and stuff. But it's kind of true. Like Latinos, Latin women are very, uh, what's the word? Uh, easy.

Well, that's, that's kind of cool, I guess. Um, should I tell stories? Um, should I give an example? Um, give an example. Um, yeah, I'll give an example. Cause this like would never happen in America. Um, yeah, definitely just some weird stuff.

Um, like I was, I was living in an apartment and like, um, um, um, my, my roommate's girlfriend brings her friend over to my apartment to like, I'd like it four o'clock in the morning on like a Friday night on a disco, you know, to like sleep with my buddy who was staying with me.
Not my roommate, but my buddy. And, uh, like that's pretty hardcore. I didn't see that in America. Anyway, she ends up like jumping in bed with me instead for some reason.
I'm like, you know, ends up having sex with me and, uh, sleeping with me for three days. Not going home for three days.

And the whole time I have a girlfriend and then my girlfriend comes and finds out and doesn't even care because she was too timing on me with some Norwegian guy like a week before and she didn't care that I knew.

And like, so that's pretty crazy. And then, um, um, this, uh, um, yeah. So, you know, like, so like they're, they're definitely a lot more jealous, but I mean, there's a definite reason cause there's kind of like, it's easier to, easier to, uh, it's more hot blooded, I guess.
Um, so anyway, yeah, not as, not as, uh, not as strict with all the rules of money and time and all that kind of stuff.
It can, they can, they can kind of, you know, let it flow, go with the flow.
So as far as, you know, using the law, utilizing the laws of synchronicity, just like whatever happens, you take it, it would, uh, definitely go well with, uh, Chilean.
And, you know, I would say maybe a lot Latin culture in general, or maybe even just underdeveloped countries in general.

Cause I know in like lots of underdeveloped countries, like Africa and stuff, India and Nepal, you know, people just like, don't really, they're not, we're looking at their times like, oh my God, oh my God, six hours have gone by.
What am I going to do? You know, like I was in Kathmandu and we'd have these Nepali guys come over and hang out with us at my, the hotel I was staying at.
The, uh, Kathmandu Centered Healing, where I learned Thai massage and they're all like, they just like sit with us all day long, just sit there.
Kind of like meditating with us, you know, and not being like, you know, so it's kind of like stoner mentality, I guess, is what they have.
And that's cool. You know, you can learn a lot from that. So that's what I think about Chile.
